about.intro: Hi, I'm Your Name. I build software for a living and tinker with hardware, type, and visual systems for fun.
paragraphs: I work on developer tools and design systems, with a soft spot for low-level rendering and weird interfaces. Most days I'm somewhere between a terminal and a sketchbook.
Outside of work I shoot 35mm film, ride bikes too far, and read more sci-fi than is strictly necessary.

contact.availability: Open to interesting work — drop a note.

uses.intro: Tools I reach for daily, and a few I just love.

scratchpad.intro: Half-formed thoughts and short notes. New ones go in content/scratchpad/ as YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md with a frontmatter title and date.

projects.title: Brutalist AI Dashboard
projects.blurb: Generative banner generator that turns any seed string into a deterministic LLM-training visualization.

projects.title: Plotter Studio
projects.blurb: SVG-first design tool for AxiDraw pen plotters, with live preview and HPGL export.

projects.title: Quiet Reader
projects.blurb: Distraction-free RSS reader. Self-hostable, keyboard-first, no tracking.

projects.title: Type Lab
projects.blurb: Variable-font playground for testing custom typefaces in real layouts.

projects.title: Field Notes API
projects.blurb: Tiny Rust HTTP server that turns a folder of markdown files into a JSON feed.

projects.title: Pico Synth
projects.blurb: Raspberry Pi Pico-based 4-voice subtractive synth with capacitive-touch keys.
